prompt: Syllabus excerpt: 'CS101 Introduction to Programming. Prerequisites: None. Credits: 3. Learning outcomes: (1) Write basic Python programs. (2) Understand control flow. (3) Use functions and modules.' Extract the course code, number of credits, and list all learning outcomes.
reference: CS101, 3 credits, outcomes: write basic Python programs; understand control flow; use functions and modules

prompt: Lecture note: 'The water cycle has four main stages: evaporation (water turns to vapour), condensation (vapour forms clouds), precipitation (rain/snow falls), and collection (water gathers in oceans/rivers).' Summarise the four stages in one sentence each.
reference: Evaporation: liquid water becomes vapour. Condensation: vapour forms clouds. Precipitation: water falls as rain or snow. Collection: water gathers in oceans and rivers.

prompt: Policy document: 'Late submissions will be penalised 10% per day up to a maximum of 50%. Extensions must be requested at least 48 hours before the deadline. Medical extensions require a doctor's certificate.' What is the maximum penalty and what is required for a medical extension?
reference: Maximum penalty is 50%; medical extension requires a doctor's certificate

prompt: Research abstract: 'We studied 120 undergraduate students across three groups. Group A (control) received no intervention. Group B received weekly tutoring. Group C received tutoring plus peer mentoring. At semester end, Group C showed a 22% improvement in grades vs Group A, and Group B showed 12%.' Which group performed best and by how much compared to control?
reference: Group C, 22% improvement over control

prompt: Reading list: 'Required: Brown (2022) Introduction to Statistics; Jones (2021) Data Analysis with Python. Recommended: Smith (2020) Statistical Thinking; Patel (2023) Machine Learning Basics.' How many required texts are listed? Name them.
reference: 2 required texts: Brown 2022 Introduction to Statistics; Jones 2021 Data Analysis with Python

prompt: Assessment schedule: 'Assignment 1: due Week 4 (20%). Assignment 2: due Week 8 (25%). Mid-term exam: Week 9 (15%). Final project: due Week 15 (30%). Participation: continuous (10%).' What is the total weighting of take-home assignments (not exams or participation)?
reference: 75% (Assignment 1 20% + Assignment 2 25% + Final project 30%)

prompt: Lab protocol: 'Step 1: Put on safety goggles and gloves. Step 2: Measure 50ml of distilled water in a beaker. Step 3: Slowly add 5g of sodium chloride and stir until dissolved. Step 4: Record the time taken to dissolve.' What safety equipment is required and what solute is used?
reference: Safety goggles and gloves; sodium chloride (NaCl)

prompt: Meeting minutes: 'Attendees: Prof. Lee, Dr. Garcia, two student representatives. Agreed: (1) Extend submission deadline to Friday. (2) Provide additional tutoring on Thursdays. (3) Review assessment rubric by end of month.' How many action items were agreed and what are they?
reference: 3 action items: extend deadline to Friday; additional Thursday tutoring; review rubric by month end

prompt: Grant abstract: 'This project requests $45,000 to develop AI-assisted reading tools for students with dyslexia. Duration: 18 months. PI: Dr. Tan. Co-I: Dr. Singh. Expected outcomes: a validated tool suite and two peer-reviewed publications.' State the budget, duration, PIs, and expected outputs.
reference: $45,000, 18 months, PI Dr. Tan Co-I Dr. Singh, validated tool suite and two publications

prompt: Timetable note: 'MATH201 meets Mondays 10-12 in Room A3 and Wednesdays 14-15 in Room B7. PHYS101 meets Tuesdays and Thursdays 09-10 in Room C2.' Which course meets for more total hours per week?
reference: MATH201 meets 3 hours per week; PHYS101 meets 2 hours per week; MATH201 has more

prompt: Ethics guidelines excerpt: 'Informed consent must be obtained from all participants. Data must be anonymised before analysis. Results must not be fabricated or selectively reported. Any conflict of interest must be declared.' List the four ethical requirements.
reference: Informed consent; data anonymisation; no fabrication or selective reporting; declare conflicts of interest

prompt: Course description: 'ENG201 Technical Writing. This course develops students ability to write clear, concise technical documents including reports, proposals, and manuals. Students will work in teams on a capstone project worth 40% of the final grade.' What capstone weighting and what types of documents are covered?
reference: 40% weighting; reports, proposals, manuals

prompt: Safety notice: 'The laboratory is closed on public holidays and weekends unless prior approval has been obtained from the lab supervisor. All visitors must sign in at reception and wear a lab coat. Eating and drinking are prohibited inside the lab.' Classify each rule as access, visitor, or conduct.
reference: Access: closed on holidays/weekends without approval. Visitor: must sign in and wear lab coat. Conduct: no eating or drinking.

prompt: Funding announcement: 'Applications open 1 March and close 31 May. Eligible applicants must be enrolled full-time and have a GPA of at least 3.5. Awards of $5,000 will be made to up to 10 students.' What are the three eligibility criteria?
reference: Enrolled full-time; GPA at least 3.5; application submitted between 1 March and 31 May

prompt: Exam instructions: 'Duration: 2 hours. Answer ALL questions in Section A (40 marks) and TWO questions from Section B (30 marks each). Calculators are permitted. Write your student ID on every page.' Summarise the structure and total marks available.
reference: Section A all questions 40 marks + 2 of Section B at 30 marks each = 100 marks total. 2 hours, calculators allowed.

prompt: Progress report excerpt: 'Milestone 1 (literature review): completed on time. Milestone 2 (data collection): delayed by 2 weeks due to ethics approval. Milestone 3 (analysis): currently on track. Milestone 4 (report writing): not yet started.' How many milestones are on track or complete?
reference: 2 (M1 complete, M3 on track)

prompt: Orientation document: 'International students must register with the International Office within 7 days of arrival. You will need: passport, visa, enrolment confirmation, and accommodation details. Office hours are Monday to Friday, 9am to 4pm.' List the required documents and the registration timeframe.
reference: Passport, visa, enrolment confirmation, accommodation details; within 7 days of arrival

prompt: Module guide: 'Week 1-3: Foundations of machine learning. Week 4-6: Supervised learning. Week 7-9: Unsupervised learning. Week 10-12: Deep learning. Week 13: Revision. Week 14: Exam.' How many weeks are devoted to supervised and unsupervised learning combined?
reference: 6 weeks (weeks 4-6 and 7-9)

prompt: Survey results summary: '65% of respondents were satisfied with online lecture quality. 48% were satisfied with assignment feedback timeliness. 72% would recommend the course to a peer. The lowest-rated item was feedback timeliness.' Which item had the highest satisfaction and which the lowest?
reference: Highest: recommend to peer 72%; lowest: feedback timeliness 48%

prompt: Job advertisement: 'Required: PhD in Computer Science or related field. 3+ years postdoctoral experience. Strong publication record. Desirable: experience with industry partnerships and grant writing.' Distinguish between required and desirable qualifications.
reference: Required: PhD, 3+ years postdoc, strong publications. Desirable: industry partnerships and grant writing experience.

prompt: Textbook chapter summary: 'Classical conditioning (Pavlov): learning by association between stimulus and response. Operant conditioning (Skinner): learning through rewards and punishments. Observational learning (Bandura): learning by watching others.' Name the three types and their theorists.
reference: Classical conditioning-Pavlov; Operant conditioning-Skinner; Observational learning-Bandura

prompt: Data glossary entry: 'Mean: arithmetic average of all values. Median: middle value when data is sorted. Mode: most frequently occurring value. Range: difference between maximum and minimum values.' What does range measure and how is it computed?
reference: Range measures spread; computed as maximum minus minimum

prompt: Programme regulations: 'Students must pass all core modules with a minimum grade of C to progress. Electives may be repeated once. A student who fails a core module twice will be referred to the Academic Progress Committee.' Under what two conditions does a student face special review?
reference: Failing a core module twice; the Academic Progress Committee reviews this

prompt: Lab report rubric: 'Introduction (15%): background and aims. Methods (20%): detailed and reproducible. Results (25%): clear tables and figures. Discussion (30%): interpretation and links to literature. Conclusion (10%): concise summary.' Which section is most heavily weighted?
reference: Discussion at 30%

prompt: Technology update notice: 'From Monday, the student portal will require two-factor authentication. Students should download the university authentication app and register their device before Sunday midnight. Those without a smartphone may collect a hardware token from IT Services.' What is the deadline for registration and what is the alternative for students without smartphones?
reference: Sunday midnight; hardware token from IT Services

prompt: Workshop description: 'This 3-hour hands-on session covers: (1) setting up a Python virtual environment, (2) installing common data science libraries, (3) loading and cleaning a CSV dataset, (4) visualising data with matplotlib.' List the four activities in order.
reference: 1. Set up virtual environment 2. Install libraries 3. Load and clean CSV 4. Visualise with matplotlib

prompt: Library notice: 'From next semester, all journal articles will be accessible through the new OpenAccess portal. Physical periodical subscriptions will be cancelled. Students requiring print copies should contact the librarian at least 2 weeks in advance.' What is changing and what must students do for print copies?
reference: Physical subscriptions cancelled; moving to OpenAccess portal; students must contact librarian 2 weeks ahead

prompt: Feedback summary: 'Positive themes: engaging lectures, supportive staff, good group work. Areas for improvement: more worked examples needed, assessment criteria could be clearer, office hours too limited.' List the positive themes and improvement areas.
reference: Positives: engaging lectures, supportive staff, good group work. Improvements: more worked examples, clearer rubrics, extended office hours

prompt: Module learning outcomes: 'On completion, students will be able to: (a) analyse primary sources critically; (b) construct evidence-based arguments; (c) write in the appropriate academic register; (d) reference sources using APA 7th edition.' Which outcome relates to citation style?
reference: Outcome (d): reference sources using APA 7th edition

prompt: Budget breakdown: 'Personnel costs: $120,000 (60%). Equipment: $40,000 (20%). Travel: $20,000 (10%). Dissemination: $20,000 (10%). Total: $200,000.' What is the largest cost category and what percentage is it?
reference: Personnel costs, 60%

prompt: Study guide note: 'To prepare for the exam: review lecture slides (weeks 1-10), complete all practice questions, re-read the textbook chapters listed in the course guide, and attend the revision session on Thursday at 2pm.' List the four recommended preparation activities.
reference: Review slides weeks 1-10; complete practice questions; re-read listed textbook chapters; attend Thursday revision session

prompt: Project brief: 'Teams of 3-4 students will design a mobile app prototype addressing a real-world community problem. Deliverables: problem statement (Week 4), prototype (Week 10), final presentation (Week 13). Each deliverable must include a peer evaluation form.' How many deliverables are there and what recurring requirement applies to all?
reference: 3 deliverables; each must include a peer evaluation form

prompt: Internship report criteria: 'Word count: 2,500-3,000 words. Structure: introduction, company overview, role description, critical reflection, conclusion. Submission: via the online portal by 11:59pm on the due date. Late penalties apply.' What are the word count limits and the required sections?
reference: 2500-3000 words; sections: introduction, company overview, role description, critical reflection, conclusion

prompt: Campus map legend: 'Red buildings: academic. Blue: administration. Green: student services. Yellow: residential. Shaded areas: under construction.' A student needs to find student counselling. What colour should they look for?
reference: Green (student services)

prompt: Research methods note: 'Qualitative research explores meanings and experiences through interviews or observation. Quantitative research tests hypotheses using numerical data and statistics. Mixed methods combine both.' When would a researcher choose quantitative over qualitative methods?
reference: When testing hypotheses using numerical data and statistical analysis

prompt: Software licence summary: 'This software is licensed for educational use only. Redistribution is prohibited. Modifications must be approved by the licensor. The licence expires 12 months after issue.' List the four licence conditions.
reference: Educational use only; no redistribution; modifications need approval; expires after 12 months

prompt: Assessment feedback: 'Strengths: clear thesis statement, effective use of evidence. Weaknesses: limited engagement with counterarguments, some in-text citations missing, conclusion too brief.' How many strengths and weaknesses were identified?
reference: 2 strengths, 3 weaknesses

prompt: Timetable change notice: 'CHEM202 tutorials have moved from Tuesday 3pm to Wednesday 11am starting Week 5. The lecture on Friday 9am remains unchanged. Students affected should update their calendars.' What changed and what stayed the same?
reference: Tutorial moved to Wednesday 11am from Week 5; Friday lecture unchanged

prompt: Handout on referencing: 'APA in-text: (Author, Year). For a direct quote add page number: (Author, Year, p.XX). For three or more authors use et al. from the first citation.' How should in-text citations differ between a paraphrase and a direct quote?
reference: Paraphrase: (Author, Year); Direct quote: (Author, Year, p.XX)

prompt: Placement handbook: 'Students are expected to work 20 hours per week. Any absences must be reported to both the host organisation and the placement coordinator within 1 hour. Students must submit a weekly reflective log by Sunday midnight.' What are the three main student obligations?
reference: Work 20 hours per week; report absences within 1 hour; submit weekly reflective log by Sunday midnight

prompt: Glossary: 'Hypothesis: a testable prediction. Variable: a measurable factor. Control group: participants not receiving the treatment. Experimental group: participants receiving the treatment.' What is the difference between the control and experimental groups?
reference: Control group receives no treatment; experimental group receives the treatment

prompt: Email from lecturer: 'Dear students, due to a timetabling conflict, next week's Thursday seminar (3pm, Room D4) is cancelled. It will be rescheduled; I will email you the new time by Friday. The Friday lecture at 10am is unaffected.' What is cancelled, what is unaffected, and by when will students be notified?
reference: Thursday 3pm seminar cancelled; Friday 10am lecture unaffected; new time by Friday

prompt: Degree requirements document: 'To graduate, students must complete 240 credits: 120 from core modules, 60 from electives, and 60 from the capstone project. A minimum GPA of 2.0 is required.' What are the three credit components?
reference: 120 core, 60 electives, 60 capstone

prompt: Workshop evaluation form instruction: 'Rate each item from 1 (strongly disagree) to 5 (strongly agree). Items 1-5 assess content, items 6-10 assess delivery, item 11 is an overall satisfaction rating.' What scale is used and what does item 11 measure?
reference: 1-5 scale; item 11 measures overall satisfaction

prompt: Crisis support notice: 'If you are experiencing academic or personal difficulty, contact Student Wellbeing at wellbeing@uni.edu or call 1800-XXX-XXX (available 24/7). For immediate emergencies, call 000.' Distinguish between the two contact options provided.
reference: Student Wellbeing for academic or personal difficulty; 000 for immediate emergencies

prompt: Conference abstract: 'This paper presents a novel algorithm for real-time anomaly detection in IoT sensor streams. Using a sliding-window approach, the algorithm achieves 94% precision and 91% recall on a benchmark dataset of 10,000 samples.' What algorithm type, performance metrics, and dataset size are mentioned?
reference: Sliding-window anomaly detection; 94% precision, 91% recall; 10,000 samples

prompt: Scholarship terms: 'The scholarship covers tuition fees for one academic year. Recipients must maintain a GPA of 3.0 or above and enrol in a minimum of 80% of available credit hours. The award is not renewable.' List the conditions for maintaining the scholarship.
reference: GPA 3.0+; enrol in at least 80% of available credits; not renewable

prompt: Onboarding checklist: '1. Set up university email. 2. Enrol in courses via the student portal. 3. Collect student ID card from admin office. 4. Attend orientation session. 5. Complete online health and safety module.' A new student has completed steps 1, 2, and 4. What remains?
reference: Steps 3 and 5: collect student ID card and complete health and safety module

prompt: Peer review instructions: 'Read the draft carefully and provide written feedback addressing: (a) clarity of argument, (b) use of evidence, (c) structure and flow, (d) grammar and spelling. Feedback should be constructive and specific. Do not rewrite sections for the author.' What four areas must peer feedback address?
reference: Clarity of argument; use of evidence; structure and flow; grammar and spelling

prompt: Invoice: 'Item 1: Lab consumables - $320. Item 2: Software licence - $450. Item 3: Conference registration - $780. Item 4: Travel reimbursement - $215. Subtotal: $1765. GST (10%): $176.50. Total due: $1941.50.' Verify the subtotal and total. Are the calculations correct?
reference: Subtotal 320+450+780+215=1765 correct; GST 176.50 correct; total 1941.50 correct
